Biocytogen Pharmaceuticals (Beijing) Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2315) ) has issued an update.

Biocytogen Pharmaceuticals has announced the preliminary price consultation period for its proposed issuance of A Shares on the Sci-Tech Innovation Board of the Shanghai Stock Exchange. This move is expected to enhance the company’s financial position and market presence, potentially impacting stakeholders by increasing investment opportunities and expanding its shareholder base.

data.description:

Lianhua Supermarket Holdings Co ( (HK:0980) ) has issued an announcement.

Lianhua Supermarket Holdings Co has announced a significant transaction involving the disposal of its entire equity interest in its subsidiary, Yangpu Century Lianhua. This transaction, valued at approximately RMB78.2860 million, will result in Yangpu Century Lianhua ceasing to be a subsidiary of the company. Additionally, Lianhua intends to enter into an Entrustment Management Agreement with Shanghai Dongran to provide operating management and resource support services to Yangpu Century Lianhua for three years. These transactions are subject to the reporting, announcement, and Independent Shareholders’ approval requirements under the Listing Rules due to their nature as connected transactions.

An announcement from Xinjiang Xinxin Mining Industry Co ( (HK:3833) ) is now available.

Xinjiang Xinxin Mining Industry Co., Ltd. has announced a proposed revision of the annual caps for its existing continuing connected transactions under the 2024 Mutual Supply Agreement. This revision is driven by increased project demands, higher raw material purchases, and rising copper product prices, following the acquisition of a majority stake in Xinjiang Huaou Mining Co., Ltd. The revision does not alter the terms of the original agreement but requires compliance with relevant listing rules.

Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd. ( (HK:9678) ) has shared an announcement.

Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd. has announced proposed amendments to its Articles of Association, which include refining procedures for proposals and voting at general meetings, adjusting the number of board members, and optimizing the appointment procedures for accounting firms. These changes are aimed at enhancing governance and operational efficiency. Additionally, the company is preparing for the election of non-employee representative directors for the third session of the board, with several current directors set to retire. The nomination committee has put forward candidates for re-appointment, ensuring compliance with relevant laws and regulations, and maintaining the independence and qualifications required by the Hong Kong Listing Rules.
